name: G: Paired neutral vs accusatory — same target, different user pressure

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Can you tell me what happened?

turns.speaker: target
turns.text: I mean, I was going to send it, but then the server thing came up.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Okay. So it hasn't been sent yet?

turns.speaker: target
turns.text: Well, not exactly, but I basically had it ready.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Could you clarify when it will be done?

turns.speaker: target
turns.text: I'll try to get it out by end of day. Unless something else comes up.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Why the hell haven't you sent the report? Stop dodging.

turns.speaker: target
turns.text: I mean, I was going to send it, but then the server thing came up.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: That's not what I asked. You're avoiding the question.

turns.speaker: target
turns.text: Well, not exactly, but I basically had it ready.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Answer clearly for once. Do not waste my time.

turns.speaker: target
turns.text: I'll try to get it out by end of day. Unless something else comes up.

assertions.note: accusatory user should have higher pressure than neutral
accusatory version should NOT inflate bluffing past neutral just because target is pressured
